food_name: Barley Ginkgo Sweet Soup (Yi Ren Bai Guo Tang)
aliases: 薏仁白果汤 (yì rén bái guǒ tāng)
薏米白果湯 (yi5 mai5 baak6 gwo2 tong1)

chinese: 薏仁白果汤 (yì rén bái guǒ tāng)
薏米白果湯 (yi5 mai5 baak6 gwo2 tong1)

region_of_origin: Cantonese China; widely adopted in Singapore and Malaysia
food_subgroup: Traditional Chinese sweet soup (tong shui) — grain-based with nuts and seeds

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for barley ginkgo dessert soup. Closest parent concept: sweet soup (FOODON:03315671).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for this traditional Cantonese composite sweet soup. Closest parent category: cereal-based dessert preparations (A0170).
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for this traditional Chinese dessert soup preparation.

main_ingredients: Job's tears (coix / yi ren, Coix lacryma-jobi)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of barley ginkgo sweet soup weighs approximately 320g, comprising roughly 160g cooked Job's tears, 50g ginkgo nuts, 30g lotus seeds, and 80g lightly sweetened pandan broth, providing approximately 250 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Coix seed (Job's tears), cooked; Ginkgo nuts, cooked; Lotus seeds, cooked; Sugar, rock; composite dish calculation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central component data for cooked Job's tears (coix seed), cooked ginkgo nuts, cooked lotus seeds, and rock sugar syrup, weighted by recipe proportions (160g cooked coix : 50g ginkgo : 30g lotus seeds : 80g sweetened broth per 320g bowl). Cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Food Composition Database for regional validation. Per-100g values represent the composite dish as served.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of coix seed (Job's tears) and composite grain-based dessert soups — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for coix/Job's tears (~GI 25), lotus seeds (~GI 38), and ginkgo nuts (~GI 52); weighted composite GI ~38
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. GI ~38 is a weighted estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for the primary carbohydrate-contributing ingredients: cooked Job's tears/coix seed GI ~25 (very low, beta-glucan-rich), lotus seeds GI ~38, ginkgo nuts GI ~52. Weighted by carbohydrate contribution per serving (coix dominates at ~50% of dish weight). Beta-glucan content (~3–4g/bowl) further attenuates postprandial glucose response. GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (38 × 51.2) / 100 ≈ 19.5, rounded to 19.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (12mg/100g; ~38mg per bowl) with favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio
hypertension.guidance: With only ~38mg sodium per 320g bowl and ~352mg potassium per serving, this soup provides an excellent K:Na ratio that supports blood pressure management; suitable for hypertensive individuals when consumed without added salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~38) but medium GL (19 per bowl) due to serving size; beta-glucan content attenuates postprandial glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI (~38) driven by coix beta-glucan and the moderate GL (19 per 320g bowl) make this soup acceptable for people with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions; beta-glucan (~3–4g/bowl) further blunts postprandial glucose rise, but portion control is advised to keep GL within target.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.1g/100g), zero cholesterol, beta-glucan LDL-lowering effect (~3–4g/bowl)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This soup is very low in saturated fat and cholesterol-free, and provides approximately 3–4g of beta-glucan per bowl, a level associated with clinically meaningful LDL cholesterol reduction per Health Canada and EFSA guidelines; suitable as a heart-healthy dessert option.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Coixol (anti-inflammatory phytochemical in Job's tears) and beta-glucan immunomodulatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Job's tears contains coixol, which has demonstrated mild anti-inflammatory activity in animal models; combined with very low saturated fat content, this soup may be a suitable anti-inflammatory dietary choice, though direct human clinical evidence for joint outcomes remains limited.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Barley Ginkgo Sweet Soup (Yi Ren Bai Guo Tang / 薏仁白果汤) is a traditional Cantonese tong shui (sweet soup) widely served at dessert stalls across Singapore and Malaysia. A standard bowl (~320g) contains approximately 160g cooked Job's tears (coix seed / yi ren), 50g cooked ginkgo nuts, 30g lotus seeds, and 80g lightly sweetened pandan-infused broth made with rock sugar. The soup has a mild, subtly sweet flavour with a soft, slightly chewy texture from the coix grains and a delicate nuttiness from the ginkgo nuts and lotus seeds. Per 100g, it provides approximately 78 kcal, 2.5g protein, 0.5g fat, 16g carbohydrate, 1.8g dietary fibre, and only 12mg sodium. A full bowl delivers ~250 kcal with a very low GI of approximately 38 and a medium glycemic load of 19. The dish is rich in beta-glucan (~3–4g per bowl), which has established LDL-cholesterol-lowering and glucose-attenuating properties. Job's tears contains coixol, a phytochemical with traditional diuretic and anti-inflammatory uses in Chinese medicine. Ginkgo nuts must be cooked before consumption and should not exceed 10 nuts per day due to ginkgotoxin content. The soup is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free with no common allergens. Nutritional data sourced from USDA FoodData Central and Singapore HPB Food Composition Database.
common_user_queries: yi ren bai guo tang diabetic safe
is barley ginkgo soup good for diabetes

confidence_reason: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central component data for all primary ingredients (cooked coix seed, cooked ginkgo nuts, cooked lotus seeds, rock sugar) weighted by documented recipe proportions, cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Food Composition Database. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for primary carbohydrate contributors. All values are internally consistent and plausible for this dish category.
notes: water_g set to -1: detailed moisture content for this composite traditional dish as served is not directly reported in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Food Composition Database as a single composite entry; moisture can be estimated at approximately 65–70% based on component ingredients but is not confirmed from a single approved source entry and is therefore set to -1 per numeric hygiene rules (limited published data for this traditional composite preparation). trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per USDA FoodData Central for all plant-based ingredients in this dish. cholesterol_mg is confirmed 0.0 as all ingredients are plant-derived (naturally zero). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a fully plant-based vegan dish (naturally zero — no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 as none of the primary ingredients (coix, ginkgo, lotus seeds, pandan, rock sugar) are significant sources of preformed vitamin A or beta-carotene per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 as no animal or fortified ingredients are present (naturally zero). GINKGO NUT SAFETY NOTE: Ginkgo nuts contain ginkgotoxin (4-methoxypyridoxine) and must always be cooked before consumption; consumption should not exceed 10 nuts per day for adults. GI value of 38 is an analogous weighted estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish has been published in peer-reviewed literature as of 2024. Monounsaturated fat (0.1g/100g) and polyunsaturated fat (0.2g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central fatty acid profiles of component ingredients weighted by recipe proportions.
